calendar.matters:
PETITION TO TERMINATE ESTATE (NO ASSETS)
Off Calendar May-18-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Peter Schwarz for Barbara Bushnell (Petitioner)

The Court questioned if there are any funds leftover after the foreclosure. Mr. Bushnell responded, "No".

Mr. Bushnell stated that the decedent died in 2010, his client came to him in 2012, that he has not had contact with him since and that he has retired his practice and he is eager to finish this case.

The Court took the petition to terminate estate under submission.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez/Nancy Wong. Bailiff: Deputy Grimaldi. (Not Reported)

PETITION TO TERMINATE ESTATE (NO ASSETS)
Off Calendar May-02-2016 Continued To May-16-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On The Filing Of Petition For Final Distribution Or Petition For Approval Of Status Report
Off Calendar Apr-12-2016 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On The Filing Of Petition For Final Distribution Or Petition For Approval Of Status Report
Off Calendar Mar-22-2016 Continued To Apr-12-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Peter Schwarz for James Morrison.

The Court asked counsel if there has been any progress. Mr. Schwarz reported that since retirement he has no support, that he tried to e-file a petition and notice but it was rejected, that it was a petition for termination and discharge, that notice was sent to the parties, that he received a call from the decedents sister Catherine Chambliss who said that she has not heard from Mr. Morrison since the funeral, that the property has been foreclosed on and that her daughter received the silver plating at the time of the funeral. Mr. Schwarz clarified that the call was received after he attempted to file the petition. The Court stated that the updated information should be in the petition. Mr. Schwarz stated that electronic filing is a problem, that his filings were rejected for several reasons and that he is not sure what the court expects him to file now. The Court stated that it is possible to e-file, that counsel could possibly file an ex parte request to not e-file, that it is not sure it would be granted, that something needs to be e-filed and that there has been progress. The status on filing a petition for final distribution or a petition for approval of a status report was continued to April 12, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 and that it is expected that a petition will be on file.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Fender. (Not Reported)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On The Filing Of Petition For Final Distribution Or Petition For Approval Of Status Report
Off Calendar Feb-23-2016 Continued To Mar-22-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Peter Schwarz for James Morrison.

Mr. Schwarz reported that there has been no progress, that he has written a letter but has not received a response and no return on the letter, that there is no evidence of foreclosure and that he has checked the internet. The Court asked if he has been able to reach any family members. Mr. Schwarz stated that the only family member he knows is in Texas, that he leaves messages but no response and that he thinks the estate should be terminated and that he should be relieved as counsel. The Court stated that counsel will need to file a notion to be relieved which requires notice, that it may prompt someone to respond and that the estate still needs to get taken care of. The status on filing a petition for final distribution or petition for approval of status report was continued to March 22, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. (Not Reported)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On The Filing Of Petition For Final Distribution Or Petition For Approval Of Status Report
Off Calendar Jan-20-2016 Continued To Feb-23-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Peter Schwartz for James Morrison.

Mr. Schwartz reported that he has had no contact with Mr. Morrison since 2012, that he has attempted to reach him via telephone and mail, that he is not sure that he has a good address for Mr. Morrison, that the property had been highly mortgaged, that he suspects that it was let go/foreclosed on but is not sure and that he himself has retired and closed his office. Mr. Schwartz further reported that he has tried to contact family members with no response, that there are no unsecured creditors, that the will mentions a tea set but he does not know what happened to it, that the property was the only other asset, that Mr. Morrison was a companion of the decedent and living at the property. The Court asked what steps had been made to contact Mr. Morrison. Mr. Schwartz stated that he has sent letters and made telephone calls and that the last contact was a letter that he sent in 2012 requesting that Mr. Morrison contact him as he owed Mr. Schwartz recording fees. The Court asked if there were any other interested parties. Mr. Schwartz stated that there were no other beneficiaries, confirmed that there were relatives that were entitled to notice and that last week he called Kathy Chambliss in Salt Lake City and left a message but has not heard back. The Court stated that if the only asset is the home and secured creditors have taken it there may not be anything else in the estate which would need to be closed out. The status on filing a petition for final distribution was continued to February 23, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for either a petition for final distribution or another way to close the estate.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Rodrin. (Not Reported)

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Apr-04-2012 Granted Per Written Order
